steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must complete the noun phrase "the most prestigious ______" and connect it to "the Nobel Prize in Literature." The full sentence should read: "...the wide range of writers to be granted what is arguably the most prestigious [blank] the Nobel Prize in Literature." The blank provides the noun that names the award, and the punctuation must correctly introduce the Nobel Prize as the specific example of that award.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘award for a person of letters;’: A semicolon must join two independent clauses. "The Nobel Prize in Literature" is a noun phrase, not an independent clause, so a semicolon is incorrect here. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘award: for a person of letters’: Placing the colon before "for a person of letters" breaks apart the noun phrase "award for a person of letters" and leaves "for a person of letters the Nobel Prize in Literature" as a fragment after the colon. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘award for a person of letters:’: This completes the noun phrase "the most prestigious award for a person of letters," then uses a colon to introduce the Nobel Prize as the specific example of that award. A colon correctly follows a complete noun phrase to introduce an elaboration. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘award; for a person of letters.’: The semicolon after "award" again requires an independent clause to follow, but "for a person of letters" is a prepositional phrase. The period also ends the sentence before "the Nobel Prize in Literature," cutting it off entirely. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
